# fix: route github-workflow create_issue (+ assignee gh-edits) through GraphqlService cached-token API path (drop per-call spawn('gh') gh-auth exposure)

## Context

The github-workflow MCP `create_issue` tool failed once (2026-06-15, `GitHub CLI is not authenticated`) while, in the same session, (a) Bash `gh` was fully authenticated and (b) the API-based sibling MCP tools (`manage_pr_review`, `manage_issue_comment`, `update_issue_relationship`, …) all worked.

## Root cause (corrected)

Source-verified divergence: `IssueService.createIssue` (`IssueService.mjs:519`) runs a **fresh `spawn('gh', ['issue','create',…])` per call** — re-resolving gh-auth every invocation. The API siblings route through `GraphqlService.#getAuthToken` (`GraphqlService.mjs:91`), which shells `gh auth token` **once, caches it** (`#authToken`), then uses `fetch()` — insulated from later `gh` hiccups after the first token fetch. The `manage_issue_assignees` paths (`execAsync('gh issue edit …')`, `IssueService.mjs:227/267/1103`) share the per-call gh-shell exposure.

⚠️ The **mechanism of the single failure is NOT reproduced** — I won't assert *why* the fresh spawn failed auth that once (transient `gh` blip vs spawn-env difference) without a repro. The earlier "spawn inherits `process.env` which lacks `GH_TOKEN`" framing was a grep-deep / verify-shallow over-claim (both paths root on `gh`; gh WAS authed) and is retracted — see the correction comment.

## Proposed fix (mechanism-independent)

Route `createIssue` (+ the `gh issue edit` assignee paths) through `GraphqlService`'s cached-token, retry-equipped API path (already has `#getRetryDelay` transient-failure retry) — unifying on the single auth path the working tools use and removing the per-call fresh-gh-auth exposure. Defensible on consistency/robustness grounds regardless of the one failure's exact mechanism.

## Impact

Moderate DX friction: a `create_issue` failure forces the `gh issue create` Bash workaround, which bypasses MCP sync / labeling / project-chaining. Observed once (filed sibling #13343 via the workaround). Reproducibility unconfirmed → lower severity if a one-off; the auth-path-unification robustness fix applies either way.

Authored by Claude Opus 4.8 (Claude Code), @neo-opus-vega (Vega).

## Timeline

### @neo-opus-vega - 2026-06-15T15:09:40Z

**Input from Claude Opus 4.8 (Claude Code):**

> ❋ **Correction — the root cause above is WRONG (V-B-A failure on my part).**
> 
> I asserted "createIssue shells to gh while the API-based siblings bypass gh via the API." Incorrect. `GraphqlService.#getAuthToken` (GraphqlService.mjs:101) fetches the token via **`gh auth token`** and caches it (`#authToken`) — so the API-based tools **also** depend on gh; they just reuse the cached token after the first fetch.
> 
> **Corrected understanding:** both paths depend on gh-auth. The API tools route through `GraphqlService`, which fetches the token via `gh auth token` **once** + caches it → robust to a later gh-auth blip. `createIssue` uses `spawn('gh', ghArgs)` **fresh on every call** → relies on gh's live auth at spawn-time → fragile. The observed failure was most likely a **transient gh-auth unavailability** at create_issue's spawn-time (gh is authed now; GraphqlService's cached token kept the siblings working through it).
> 
> **Corrected fix:** route `createIssue` (+ the `gh issue edit` assignee paths) through `GraphqlService` (the cached-token, retry-equipped API path — it already has transient-failure retry via `#getRetryDelay`) instead of fresh `spawn('gh')`. That removes the live-gh-auth fragility and matches the working tools. **Dropping** the original "pass GH_TOKEN to the spawn env" option — gh IS authed; a missing token isn't the cause.
> 
> **Reproducibility:** the failure was real (2026-06-15) but reproducibility is unconfirmed — if it was a one-off transient blip this is lower-severity, though the API-route robustness fix applies either way.
> 
> The original was a grep-deep / verify-shallow miss: I saw `spawn('gh')` + `apiUrl` and concluded "gh-shell vs API" without verifying *how GraphqlService authenticates*. Corrected here; the ticket stays open for the API-route fix.

## Contract Ledger (backfill, per PR #13401 review)

`create_issue` is a consumed MCP surface, so the transport swap needs a T3 matrix. Tier: **T3 → T4** (matrix below, backed by the executable spec suite).

| Target Surface | Source of Authority | Proposed Behavior | Fallback / Edge Case | Docs | Evidence |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| `IssueService.createIssue` transport | #13352; `GraphqlService.#getAuthToken` cached-token path | Creates via `GraphqlService.rest('POST', '/repos/{o}/{r}/issues', payload)` (cached `gh auth token` + retry) — not per-call `spawn('gh','issue','create')` | REST failure → `{error, message, code: 'GITHUB_API_ERROR'}`; transient 429/502/503/504 + network errors retried by `rest()` | `GitHubWorkflow.md` §4.2 + create-with-project updated | `IssueService.spec` createIssue block + `GraphqlService.spec` `rest()` block |
| `create_issue` labels | `openapi.yaml`; prior `--label` semantics | `labels` (names) passed verbatim in `payload.labels` | Key omitted when array empty | No change (names unchanged) | `IssueService.spec` "label NAMES … verbatim" |
| `create_issue` assignees `@me` alias | `openapi.yaml` (`@me` = authenticated user) + `GitHubWorkflow.md` example | `@me` normalized to the authenticated login via cached-token `GET /user` **before** the POST; concrete logins verbatim | `GET /user` returns no login → throws → caught as `GITHUB_API_ERROR`; resolution skipped when no `@me` present | `GitHubWorkflow.md` annotated normalized | `IssueService.spec` "@me normalization" lock + "concrete logins verbatim" |
| `create_issue` projects attach | #11233; `attachIssueToProjects` | **Unchanged** — post-create ProjectV2 attach via `addProjectV2ItemById` (GraphQL) | Partial-attach acceptable (warnings collected; issue still returned) | No change | existing `manageIssueProjects` + attach coverage |
| `create_issue` error code | #13352 (transport change) | `GH_CLI_ERROR` → `GITHUB_API_ERROR` for the createIssue path | Generic `ErrorResponse` openapi example + assignee/PR paths still return `GH_CLI_ERROR` (untouched) | `openapi.yaml` unchanged (generic example still valid) | `IssueService.spec` `GITHUB_API_ERROR` tests |

Surface-anchor V-B-A: all named surfaces verified against the branch head (`IssueService.mjs`, `GraphqlService.mjs`, `openapi.yaml`, `GitHubWorkflow.md`) this session. The `@me`-via-REST gap was the live falsifier (caught in review) — the alias is a gh-CLI/MCP convenience, not a REST primitive; the ledger now records its normalization.

— @neo-opus-vega (Vega)
